key_questions: Define the theme precisely: Evidence-first underwriting
Where does value accrue (value chain) and what are the capture mechanisms?
Who benefits vs gets left behind, and what are falsifiers and adoption signposts?

sources.title: Internal: v2 research contracts and quality gates
excerpt_or_key_points: Defines contracts for citations, numeric provenance, structure, and repair loop behavior.

sources.title: SEC XBRL time-series (AAPL)
excerpt_or_key_points: Fixture: time-series extract representing SEC XBRL submissions (for offline demo).

blocks.text: This theme memo is constrained to collected evidence; unsupported claims are marked as assumptions or omitted.
A research workflow that treats evidence as a reusable library and produces auditable investment theses via contracts.
Assumption: the evidence library will expand to include Tier one filings and Tier three transcripts for all universe members.

sections.title: What It Is / Isn't + Why Now

blocks.text: Contracts prevent generic prose from shipping by enforcing citations, numeric provenance, and section structure.
Why now: agentic systems without governance drift; fail-closed gates make research reproducible and auditable.
Assumption: the incremental time cost of gates is offset by reduced downstream rework and higher trust.
Value chain mapping should be tied to primary sources; missing sources are recorded in the appendix.
Value accrues to systems that turn evidence into decisions with low contradiction and high auditability.
Assumption: buyer preference will shift toward auditable workflows as regulation and model risk management mature.
Capture mechanisms must be tied to evidence; scorecards without sources are opinions.
Mechanisms: distribution control, workflow embedding, governance, and switching costs.
Assumption: monetization consolidates around workflow layers rather than generalized capability providers.
Beneficiary claims require evidence per name; this run records missing coverage explicitly.
Beneficiaries are names with primary evidence coverage and durable capture mechanisms; left behind lack either.
Assumption: procurement and compliance constraints will be a durable differentiator in adoption.
Catalyst calendars must be backed by dated sources; this run does not include those sources.
Treat catalysts as information releases that update scenario weights.
Assumption: adoption milestones appear in filings and transcripts before they appear in price tape.
Falsifiers should be measurable and monitored via the evidence library.
The bear case is that the perceived edge is not durable: economics compress, execution misses accumulate, and the market reprices the multiple to reflect lower confidence.
Assumption: a monitoring dashboard will track evidence freshness, contradictions, and KPI deltas per name.
The version two gates fail closed when sourcing or numeric provenance is missing.
Expression: stage exposure (pilot/core) only after evidence coverage and falsifiers are defined.
Assumption: skipping low-evidence names improves risk-adjusted outcomes in a broad opportunity set.

exhibits.title: Value Chain Map (Value chain)
exhibits.question: Where does value accrue along the workflow from evidence to decision?
data_summary: Primary sources -> normalization -> analyzers -> skeptical review -> memo compiler -> portfolio decision.
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Value accrues to systems that make evidence reusable and decisions auditable.

exhibits.title: Capture Scorecard (Capture scorecard)
exhibits.question: Which mechanisms drive capture and how do we score them?
data_summary: Distribution, workflow embedding, governance, and switching cost are scored only when evidence exists.
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Scorecards without evidence are opinions, not research.

exhibits.title: Adoption Dashboard (Adoption dashboard)
exhibits.question: What adoption signposts should we monitor?
data_summary: Filings: KPI deltas and disclosure; transcripts: customer and pipeline signals; price: regime shifts.
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Dashboards turn themes into monitorable systems.

exhibits.title: Catalyst Calendar (Catalyst calendar)
exhibits.question: What dated events can change scenario weights?
data_summary: Earnings cycles, major product releases, and regulatory deadlines (sources pending).
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: A calendar is only as good as its dated sources.

exhibits.title: Risk Heatmap (Risk heatmap)
exhibits.question: Where do the biggest failure modes sit across mechanism and time?
data_summary: Execution risk, regulation risk, and commoditization risk require explicit falsifiers.
exhibits.takeaway: Takeaway: Heatmaps are useful only when paired with triggers that change posture.

evidence_table.claim: Contracts enforce fail-closed rules for citations, numeric provenance, and structure.

whats_missing: Theme-specific primary sources for every universe member (filings and transcripts).
Dated catalyst sources to populate the calendar with evidence-backed events.
Quantitative adoption dashboard once KPI sources are ingested for all names.
